name: wiki-changelog
description: Analyzes git commit history and generates structured changelogs categorized by change type. Use when the user asks about recent changes, wants a changelog, or needs to understand what changed in the repository.
capabilities: Analyzes git commit history to extract all commits with dates, authors, and messages
Groups commits by time period: recent commits (last 7 days) and older commits
Categorizes commits by type: Features, Fixes, Refactoring, Docs, Config, Dependencies, Breaking Changes
Generates concise, user-facing descriptions using project terminology
Highlights breaking changes prominently with migration notes
author: sickn33
securitySummary: Static analysis flagged 4 high-risk patterns related to 'weak cryptographic algorithm' at lines 3, 22, 27, and 32. This is a FALSE POSITIVE - the skill contains no cryptographic code. The analyzer misidentified common words in the markdown documentation. The skill is a simple changelog generator that reads git log data and produces structured output. No network calls, no external commands, no file writes, no environment access.
valueStatement: Manually compiling changelogs from git history is time-consuming and error-prone. This skill automatically analyzes commit history and generates structured, categorized changelogs organized by change type and time period.
limitations: Only works with git repositories - requires access to git history
Cannot determine change impact without context from commit messages
Depends on well-written commit messages for accurate categorization
Does not generate release artifacts or publish changelogs automatically

useCases.title: Generate Release Notes
useCases.description: Quickly produce release notes by analyzing recent commits and categorizing all changes since the last release.

useCases.title: Track Project Activity
useCases.description: Understand what has changed in a project over time by viewing a structured summary of recent development activity.

useCases.title: Onboard Team Members
useCases.description: Help new team members understand recent changes and the current state of the project through organized changelogs.

promptTemplates.title: Basic Changelog Request
promptTemplates.prompt: Use the wiki-changelog skill to generate a changelog for this repository. What changes have been made recently?
promptTemplates.scenario: User wants to know recent changes

promptTemplates.title: Release Notes Request
promptTemplates.prompt: Generate a changelog for version X.X.X release. Include all features, bug fixes, and any breaking changes.

promptTemplates.title: Weekly Summary
promptTemplates.prompt: Analyze the git history and provide a weekly summary of all changes, grouped by category like features, fixes, and documentation updates.

promptTemplates.title: Breaking Changes Report
promptTemplates.prompt: Generate a changelog that highlights all breaking changes and includes migration notes for upgrading.

bestPractices: Ensure commit messages follow a consistent convention (Conventional Commits recommended) for accurate categorization
Review generated changelogs before publishing to ensure terminology matches project standards
Use the skill regularly (weekly or before each release) to maintain up-to-date changelogs
antiPatterns: Do not rely solely on automated categorization - verify that commits are grouped correctly
Avoid using vague commit messages like 'fix', 'update', 'changes' which reduce changelog usefulness
Do not skip reviewing breaking changes - always verify migration notes are accurate

faq.question: Does this skill write files to my repository?
faq.answer: No. This skill only reads git history and generates changelog output. It does not modify any files.

faq.question: How does the skill categorize commits?
faq.answer: The skill analyzes commit messages and classifies them based on keywords: Features (feat, add), Fixes (fix, bug), Refactoring (refactor), Docs (docs), Config (config), Dependencies (deps), and Breaking (break).

faq.question: Can I customize the categories?
faq.answer: Currently the categories are predefined. You can modify the skill code to add custom categories if needed.

faq.question: What if my commit messages are not descriptive?
faq.answer: The quality of the changelog depends on commit message quality. Encourage team members to write descriptive commit messages for better results.

faq.question: Does this work with git submodules?
faq.answer: The skill analyzes the main repository history. Submodule changes would need to be tracked separately.

faq.question: Can I generate changelogs for specific time periods?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill groups commits into daily (last 7 days) and weekly (older) periods by default. You can request specific date ranges in your prompt.
